paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human; non-immune system; rat liver (hepatocytes/MAFLD model), no T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: AHR deficiency exacerbates hepatic cholesterol accumulation by inhibiting bile acid synthesis.
paper_assessments.rationale: Hepatic lipid/bile acid metabolism in AHR-knockout rats; no T cell or TCR content.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human and non-human; tumor models with Tregs, CD8+ T cells and tumor-associated macrophages
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: AHR drives generation of Tregs and PD-1 up-regulation in CD8+ T cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: AHR blockade (perturbation) reverses Treg-mediated immunosuppression and PD-1-linked checkpoint response, but no proximal TCR-signalling readout.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: PCB mixtures act through AhR to dysregulate macrophage polarization, driving a pro-inflammatory phenotype.
paper_assessments.rationale: AhR-dependent macrophage polarization only; no T cells or TCR inhibition addressed.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human; intestinal infection model, mixed immune response, no TCR-specific data
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Prolonged duration of AHR activation alone does not explain TCDD toxicity.
paper_assessments.rationale: General AHR immune effects in intestinal infection; no TCR-inhibitory T cell phenotype demonstrated.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human; mouse bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells (non-immune)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: IS inhibits early osteogenesis of BMSCs through the AhR/Hes1 pathway.
paper_assessments.rationale: AhR-mediated osteogenesis in stem cells; no T cell or TCR relevance.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: not applicable; review of AHR biology in cancer
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: AHR's context-specific activity is influenced by multiple contributing factors.
paper_assessments.rationale: General AHR review; no specific primary TCR-inhibition evidence, cannot support tier independently.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human; primary T cells and T cell lines
paper_assessments.phenotype: proximal TCR signalling inhibition or attenuation
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Ser325 phosphorylation enhances PTPN22's inhibitory effect on TCR signaling.
paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns PTPN22, not AHR; strong TCR-inhibition data but wrong regulator, so irrelevant here.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: human and non-human; keratinocytes/non-hematopoietic cells in skin inflammation
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: AhR activation reduced disease severity, while its deficiency exacerbated inflammation.
paper_assessments.rationale: AhR dampens skin inflammation via non-hematopoietic keratinocytes; not TCR inhibition in T cells.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: not applicable; review of TCR repertoire selection for immunotherapy
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: Review explores using patient TCR repertoire data to select therapeutic T cells.
paper_assessments.rationale: TCR repertoire/engineering review; no AHR content or TCR-inhibitory mechanism.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: non-human; mouse primary CD4+ T cells (AhR+/+ vs AhR-/-), effector polarization
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: AhR activation down-regulated Il17a and Rorc, suggesting a role in suppressing inflammatory responses.
paper_assessments.rationale: Causal (AhR-/-) but phenotype is differentiation/gene expression and Treg induction, not TCR signalling inhibition.

parsed.tier_rationale: PMC7419300 is a primary study with direct perturbational evidence (AHR blockade) that AHR promotes an inhibitory checkpoint/Treg suppressive axis, including PD-1 up-regulation in CD8+ T cells, but it lacks a proximal TCR-signalling readout, so it is relevant-but-incomplete rather than direct TCR inhibition. PMC3412388 adds causal AhR-/- evidence of immunosuppression/Treg-associated differentiation. No primary study demonstrates direct causal AHR effects on proximal TCR signalling or TCR-linked anergy with a mechanistic TCR readout, so strong is not met; between tiers the lower is chosen but a relevant primary causal inhibitory-checkpoint study supports moderate.
